title: Tiny Powerhouses Get Their Day
story: Beans, lentils, and chickpeas take center stage on February 10 for World Pulses Day! These tiny nutritional powerhouses get celebrated worldwide as people share pulse-based recipes, cook colorful bean dishes, and learn about how these crops help feed the planet while being good for soil and the environment.
bonus_fact: Pulses can actually improve soil by adding nitrogen naturally!

title: Rain or Shine, It's Umbrella Time!
story: Every February 10, people celebrate National Umbrella Day by showing off their favorite rain shields! Some folks carry colorful umbrellas even when it's sunny outside, just for fun. Others post photos of their coolest umbrella collections on social media. Many people use this day to buy a new umbrella or finally fix that broken one sitting in their closet. It's the perfect time to appreciate these handy inventions that keep us dry!
activity_question: What do people often do with umbrellas on National Umbrella Day?
activity_choices: Carry them even when it's sunny
activity_answer: Carry them even when it's sunny
follow_up_question: What's the most creative way you could decorate an umbrella?

title: Cream Cheese Brownie Bliss Day
story: Gooey chocolate brownies get a creamy makeover every February 10 on National Cream Cheese Brownie Day! Bakers across the country swirl tangy cream cheese into their brownie batter, creating marbled masterpieces that look like edible art. Some people make cheesecake-brownie hybrids by layering cream cheese on top, while others fold chunks right into the mix. Bakeries often feature special cream cheese brownie flavors, and home bakers love posting their swirled creations on social media. The combination creates the perfect balance of rich chocolate and smooth, slightly tart cream cheese that makes regular brownies seem boring!
activity_question: What do bakers swirl into brownies to celebrate this holiday?
bonus_fact: The cream cheese creates a marbled pattern that makes each brownie look unique!

title: Cozy Up for National Flannel Day!
story: People across the country wrap themselves in their softest flannel shirts, pajamas, and blankets every February 10 for National Flannel Day! This cozy holiday celebrates the warm, fuzzy fabric that keeps us comfortable during chilly winter days. Families wear matching flannel outfits, post pictures of their favorite flannel gear on social media, and some even have flannel-themed parties. Schools sometimes let students wear pajamas to class if they're made of flannel. It's the perfect excuse to stay extra comfy all day long!
activity_question: What do people often wear to celebrate National Flannel Day?
follow_up_question: What's your favorite flannel item to wear when you want to feel extra cozy?

title: Don't Cry Over Spilled Milk Day
story: Nobody likes cleaning up messy spills, but February 10 is National Don't Cry Over Spilled Milk Day! This holiday reminds us not to get upset about small accidents or mistakes that can't be changed. People celebrate by sharing stories about times they worried too much about little problems. Some families purposely spill a tiny bit of milk at breakfast and laugh it off together. Others post funny photos of their kitchen mishaps online with cheerful messages. It's a day to practice letting go of stress and remembering that most problems aren't as big as they seem at first.
activity_question: How do some families celebrate this holiday at breakfast?
activity_choices: They spill a little milk on purpose and laugh about it
They drink chocolate milk instead of regular milk
They eat cereal without any milk at all
They wear special shirts that say 'No Crying'
activity_answer: They spill a little milk on purpose and laugh about it
follow_up_question: What's a small mistake you made that seemed big at the time but wasn't really?
